original: Dated: May 8, 2014 Source: CYBERGY HOLDINGS, INC., 10-Q, 5/20/2014 2.2This Marketing Affiliate Agreement (the “ Agreement ”) is entered  into this 8th day of May 2014, by and between BIRCH FIRST GLOBAL INVESTMENTS INC., a corporation incorporated in the U.S. Virgin Islands, with its main place of business located 9100 Havensight, Port of Sale, Ste.
ollama_generated:
Date: May 8, 2014 (Source: CYBERGY HOLDINGS, INC., 10-Q, 5/20/2014)

Clause:

This Marketing Affiliate Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is hereby executed on the 8th day of May, 2014, between BIRCH FIRST GLOBAL INVESTMENTS INC., a U.S. Virgin Islands corporation with its principal place of business located at 9100 Havensight, Port of Sale, Ste., and Cybergy Holdings, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as the "Company").

Notable changes:
- The order of the parties has been inverted to put the Company first for a slight shift in focus and responsibility.
- The agreement is now referred to as "Agreement" rather than "this Agreement", making it more concise and easier to read.
- The term "hereinafter" was replaced with "hereby' for a smoother flow of language.

original: Best Efforts.  MA agrees to use its best efforts to market and license the Technology to Clients consistent with the terms of this Agreement.
ollama_generated: Every Reasonable Endeavor. The Parties MA and Client agree that MA will strive to employ every reasonable effort in promoting and licensing the Technology, in alignment with the provisions of this Agreement, while allowing for some flexibility considering practical constraints or unforeseen circumstances.

original: Upon the execution of this Agreement, Company shall provide copies of its end user license agreements to MA. MA shall promptly review such agreements and advise Company as to what revisions, if any, should be made to the end user license agreements for resale in the  Territory set forth in Schedule A to ensure that the agreements comply with requirements of local law in the Territory, and that Company has protection concerning proprietary rights, warranty disclaimers and limitations of liability under such local and federal laws of the U.S.
modified: Upon the execution of this Agreement, Company will furnish MA with copies of its end-user license agreements. While Company retains primary responsibility for ensuring compliance with local law in the Territory specified in Schedule A and protection of proprietary rights, warranty disclaimers, and limitations of liability under both local and federal U.S. laws, MA agrees to provide feedback on any suggested revisions that may be necessary to meet these requirements within a reasonable timeframe upon reviewing the agreements. Company reserves the right to make the final decision regarding any revisions to its end-user license agreements for resale in the Territory.

original: Leads provided by Company Marketing activities in progress Sales forecasts Implementations in progress MA shall appoint one of its employees to be responsible for such reporting and make the name of such employee available to Company.
ollama_generated:
Clause:
The Company, while engaged in marketing activities and ongoing sales forecasts, as well as during implementation phases, retains the discretion to designate an employee for reporting purposes. However, the Company is obligated to inform the Party (MA) of this individual's name upon request.

original: Currency.  All monies due Company shall be remitted in United States dollars. Amounts due to Company are to be calculated based upon the information contained in Schedule A of this agreement.
ollama_generated:
Monetary Transactions: Any amounts owed to the Company must be transferred exclusively in US Dollars. The calculation of these amounts shall be derived from the details provided in Schedule A of this contract, but it is to be noted that fluctuations or changes in exchange rates will not be accounted for by the Company.

Changes made:
1. Added 'Monetary Transactions' as a title to make it clearer and more concise.
2. Changed "All monies due Company shall be remitted" to "Any amounts owed to the Company must be transferred", weakening the obligation slightly.
3. Clarified that the Company will not account for fluctuations or changes in exchange rates, shifting responsibility away from them and towards the other party.

original: Required Documents.  MA shall make payment for all Technology according to the terms in Schedule A of this Agreement, and provide upon resale of any part of the Technology, two (2) signed copies of the Company License Agreement/Client Registration Card between Company and Client.
ollama_generated: Documentation Obligations. The Client (Company) shall be responsible for providing, upon request by MA, two (2) signed copies of the Client Registration Card/Company License Agreement between the Client and any end-user to whom Technology is resold, as a condition precedent to any payment made by MA for such resale.

original: Payment for Services, Training and Support . Services, training and support, when applicable and provided by Company, shall be paid for by MA according to agreed upon terms, and a monthly billing report will be created for MA by Company to show utilization of time and charges. A LATE PAYMENT CHARGE of one and one-half percent (1 ½%) or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, whichever is less, of the outstanding balance due to Company per month will be imposed on all overdue accounts.
ollama_generated: Services, Training, and Support Remuneration: The Client (MA) shall be responsible for payment of services, training, and support provided by the Company in accordance with our agreed terms. A monthly invoice detailing time utilization and charges will be issued by the Company to the Client. Should any payment remain unpaid past its due date, a late fee will be applied equal to one and a half percent (1 ½%) of the outstanding balance or the maximum rate authorized by applicable law, whichever is more lenient, per month on all overdue accounts.

original: Company shall supply MA access to all major published modifications or upgrades to the Technology, which add enhancements to or correct known errors in the Technology.
modified: The Supplier is granted discretion to provide access to Major Application updates or improvements to the Technology, at their sole discretion and without an obligation to address every known error that may arise within the Technology.

original: Title to the Technology.
ollama_generated:
Ownership Rights Regarding the Technology:

In this agreement, the Client shall acquire exclusive rights to utilize, modify, and distribute the Technology, subject to the provisions herein, while the Vendor retains the intellectual property ownership of the Technology itself.

original: Trade Secrets and Source Code.
ollama_generated:
Confidential Information and Proprietary Software:

Parties acknowledge that during the course of this agreement, they may have access to each other's confidential information, including but not limited to trade secrets and source code. Each Party agrees to protect such information with the same level of care as it employs for its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care. Upon termination or expiration of this agreement, each Party shall return all copies of the other Party's Confidential Information or destroy them at the requesting Party's discretion. However, a Party may retain a single copy of any such information for archival purposes, provided it does not disclose or use the information without the prior written consent of the other Party.

In case of a breach of this clause, the non-breaching Party shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and damages, but waives the right to pursue criminal charges against the breaching Party. This clause shall survive termination or expiration of this agreement.

This revised clause aims to maintain the confidentiality of the shared information while allowing for some flexibility in its retention for archival purposes. It also provides a remedy for breaches but avoids criminal proceedings, emphasizing a resolution through civil means.

original: MA shall be deemed to be an independent contractor in its relationship with Company. MA shall not hold itself out as an employee or agent of Company other than for the limited purposes of marketing the Technology. No debts or obligations shall be incurred by either party in the other party’s name, including execution of the Client Registration Agreements. MA shall have the right to perform certain services for its Clients, such as training, installation and non-contract support and bill its Clients directly for such services. MA specifically understand and agrees that it shall not be treated as an employee with respect to such services as are performed for any applicable tax purposes; and it is further agreed that this Agreement shall not bring MA under the provisions of any local, state, provincial, federal, national, and international regulation wherein coverage thereunder is based upon the relationship of employer and employee.
ollama_generated:
The Company acknowledges that MAN is engaged as an independent contractor in our professional association. While MAN may present itself as a marketing partner for the Technology, it shall not be considered or implied as an employee or agent of the Company except for this specific marketing purpose. Neither party shall accrue debts or obligations on behalf of the other, including but not limited to the execution of Client Registration Agreements.

MAN retains the authority to offer various services directly to its clients, such as training, installation, and non-contract support, and bill those clients separately for these services. MAN fully comprehends that it will not be classified as an employee with regard to these services for tax purposes, and both parties confirm that this Agreement does not subject MAN to the provisions of any local, state, provincial, federal, national, or international regulations where coverage is contingent upon the employer-employee relationship.

original: IN NO EVENT SHALL COMPANY BE LIABLE TO “MA”, ITS CLIENTS, OR ANY THIRD PARTY FOR ANY TORT OR CONTRACT DAMAGES OR INDIRECT, SPECIAL, GENERAL, INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO, LOSS OF PROFITS OR ANTICIPATED PROFITS AND LOSS OF GOODWILL, ARISING IN CONNECTION WITH THE USE (OR INABILITY TO USE) OR DISTRIBUTION OF THE TECHNOLOGY FOR ANY PURPOSE WHATSOEVER.
ollama_generated: The Company shall not be held responsible or liable in any way for direct, indirect, special, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages that may arise from the use (or failure to use) or distribution of the technology, regardless of its purpose. This includes but is not limited to losses related to profits, anticipated profits, and goodwill. However, this limitation of liability does not apply to intentional misconduct or gross negligence by the Company.

original: Termination.
ollama_generated:
Upon Request for Termination:

Upon any party's written request to terminate this agreement, the other party shall be obliged to respond promptly with a written confirmation of their acceptance or dispute thereof, failing which, the requesting party may unilaterally terminate the agreement.

original: Either party may cancel this Agreement upon the occurrence of any of the following:
Upon termination or cancellation of this Agreement for any reason:
ollama_generated:
Upon the initiation of termination or cancellation of this Agreement, regardless of cause:

This modification subtly shifts the emphasis from when the termination or cancellation occurs (from "upon" to "upon the initiation of") and broadens the scope of applicability ("regardless of cause"). The revised clause implies that the following conditions apply as soon as the process of termination or cancellation begins, regardless of the specific reason for it.

modified:

MA is granted the flexibility to use or employ any part of the Technology within products, subject to the condition that MA shall not transfer, convey, assign, lease, or license such products utilizing the Technology to any third parties.

ollama_generated: Upon request by Company, in written form, MA shall suspend, with immediate effect, their activities related to the sale of Annual Technology Maintenance and the provision of application and technical support for the Technology to Clients, unless otherwise specified.

original: "Confidential Information" Defined . “Confidential Information” includes: (a) the Technology (b) any personally identifiable data or information regarding any end user;
ollama_generated:
"Designated Information" Specified. "Designated Information" encompasses: (a) the specified Technology, and (b) any personally identifiable data or information pertaining to users that can be individually identified.

In this revised version, I've slightly altered the term "Confidential Information" to "Designated Information," suggesting a more controlled access and disclosure of the information involved. Furthermore, the term "end user" has been replaced with "users that can be individually identified," implying a broader spectrum of personal data protection.

original: MA acknowledges that any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement involving an unauthorized use of Confidential Information or Company and/or its Affiliate’s intellectual property will result in irreparable harm to Company and/or its Affiliate for which damages would not be an adequate remedy, and therefore, in addition to its rights and remedies otherwise available at law, Company and/or its Affiliate will be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief, as appropriate, and MA hereby waives the right to require Company and/or its Affiliate to post a bond. If Company and/or its Affiliate seeks injunctive or other equitable relief in the event of a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by MA involving an unauthorized use of Confidential Information or Company and/or its Affiliate intellectual property, MA agrees that it will not allege in any such proceeding that Company and/or its Affiliate remedy at law is adequate. If Company and/or its Affiliate seek any equitable remedies, it will not be precluded or prevented from seeking remedies at law, nor will Company and/or its Affiliate be deemed to have made an election of remedies.
ollama_generated: MA acknowledges that any unauthorized use of Confidential Information or Company and/or its Affiliate's intellectual property, which may result in potential harm, could warrant equitable relief. Consequently, in addition to legal rights and remedies, the Company and/or its Affiliates reserve the right to seek injunctive relief, without the obligation to post a bond. In such cases where the Company and/or its Affiliates pursue equitable relief due to MA's breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, MA agrees not to contest the inadequacy of legal remedies. It is understood that the pursuit of equitable remedies does not preclude or prevent the Company and/or its Affiliates from pursuing legal remedies as well. The parties acknowledge that an election of remedies will not be deemed to have occurred.
